meta.name: End-of-Day Reflection Tree
meta.description: A deterministic reflection tool walking employees through Locus, Orientation, and Radius axes.

nodes.text: Good evening. Before you close your laptop — let's spend five minutes with today.
If today were a weather report, what was it?
options: Clear skies — things went my way
Partly cloudy — mixed results
Stormy — felt out of control
Foggy — couldn't get traction
answer=Clear skies — things went my way:A1_Q_HIGH
answer=Partly cloudy — mixed results:A1_Q_HIGH
answer=Stormy — felt out of control:A1_Q_LOW
answer=Foggy — couldn't get traction:A1_Q_LOW

nodes.text: You said "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When things went well today, what do you think drove it?
options: I prepared or planned ahead
I adapted when the situation shifted
The team or someone else made it work
It just happened to go well — timing
answer=I prepared or planned ahead:A1_R_INT_STRONG
answer=I adapted when the situation shifted:A1_R_INT_STRONG
answer=The team or someone else made it work:A1_R_EXT_MILD
answer=It just happened to go well — timing:A1_R_EXT_MILD

nodes.text: You said "{A1_OPEN.answer}". When things got hard, what was your first move?
options: I looked for what I could still control
I pushed through even without a clear plan
I waited for someone else to make a call
I got stuck replaying what went wrong
answer=I looked for what I could still control:A1_R_INT_MILD
answer=I pushed through even without a clear plan:A1_R_INT_MILD
answer=I waited for someone else to make a call:A1_R_EXT_STRONG
answer=I got stuck replaying what went wrong:A1_R_EXT_STRONG

nodes.text: Think of one moment today when something didn't go as expected. Who or what did you focus on first?
options: What I could have done differently
What the other person should have done
The circumstances that made it hard
How to fix it and move on
answer=What I could have done differently:BRIDGE_1_2_INT
answer=How to fix it and move on:BRIDGE_1_2_INT
answer=What the other person should have done:BRIDGE_1_2_EXT
answer=The circumstances that made it hard:BRIDGE_1_2_EXT

nodes.text: You see your hand in what happened today. That's not arrogance — it's agency. You stayed in the driver's seat even when the road wasn't smooth.
Even on a hard day, your instinct was to look for a handhold — something you could actually influence. That's more agency than it might feel like in the moment.
You gave credit to others or circumstances. That's honest. The question worth sitting with: was there a moment today where your choice shaped the outcome — even a small one?

Hard days pull attention outward — to what others did or didn't do, to circumstances that weren't fair. That's a very human response. Before we move on: somewhere in today, you made a call. What was it?
You've been thinking about how you handled today. Now let's shift — from how you navigated things, to what you actually gave.
You've been thinking about what today did to you. Let's flip the lens — from what you received, to what you gave.
Think about one interaction you had today — with a colleague, a manager, a report, or a client. Which of these best captures it?
options: I helped someone work through something
I shared knowledge or taught something
I felt my effort wasn't noticed or rewarded
I felt frustrated others weren't doing their part
answer=I helped someone work through something:A2_Q_CONTRIB
answer=I shared knowledge or taught something:A2_Q_CONTRIB
answer=I felt my effort wasn't noticed or rewarded:A2_Q_ENTITLE
answer=I felt frustrated others weren't doing their part:A2_Q_ENTITLE

nodes.text: When you helped or taught today — what was driving it?
options: It was the right thing to do for the team
I genuinely enjoy helping people grow
I hoped it would be noticed or appreciated
It felt easier than having the work done badly
answer=It was the right thing to do for the team:A2_R_CONTRIB_PURE
answer=I genuinely enjoy helping people grow:A2_R_CONTRIB_PURE
answer=I hoped it would be noticed or appreciated:A2_R_CONTRIB_MIXED
answer=It felt easier than having the work done badly:A2_R_CONTRIB_MIXED

nodes.text: When you felt your effort wasn't being matched or noticed — what did you do with that feeling?
options: Let it go and focused on my own work
Brought it up directly with the person
Mentioned it to someone else
It stayed with me and affected my focus
answer=Let it go and focused on my own work:A2_R_ENTITLE_MILD
answer=Brought it up directly with the person:A2_R_ENTITLE_MILD
answer=Mentioned it to someone else:A2_R_ENTITLE_STRONG
answer=It stayed with me and affected my focus:A2_R_ENTITLE_STRONG

nodes.text: Looking back at today — which statement feels more true?
options: I'm most aware of what I put in
I'm most aware of what I got back
Both are pretty balanced in my mind
I wasn't tracking either — I was just surviving today
answer=I'm most aware of what I put in:BRIDGE_2_3_CONTRIB
answer=Both are pretty balanced in my mind:BRIDGE_2_3_CONTRIB
answer=I'm most aware of what I got back:BRIDGE_2_3_ENTITLE
answer=I wasn't tracking either — I was just surviving today:BRIDGE_2_3_ENTITLE

nodes.text: You gave something today without keeping score. That's rare. Genuine contribution — the kind that isn't watching the clock for recognition — is exactly what good teams are built on.

You helped — and that counts. It's worth noticing that a part of you was also tracking whether it would be seen. Most of us do this. The question isn't whether you felt that, but whether it drove the decision to help.
The frustration you felt today was real. What's interesting is what you did with it — you either let it go or addressed it directly. That takes more self-awareness than it sounds.
When we feel under-recognized, the feeling is real — but it can also narrow our focus to what we're owed. That energy tends to leak into performance without us noticing. Worth watching.
You've been thinking about what you gave today. One more shift — from who you gave it to, to how wide your circle of concern actually stretched.
You've been thinking about what today felt like from the inside. Let's widen the lens — how much of today were you thinking about others?
When you think about today's biggest challenge — who was in the frame?
options: Mostly just me — it was my problem to solve
My immediate team — we were in it together
A specific colleague who had it harder than me
The people our work ultimately serves
answer=Mostly just me — it was my problem to solve:A3_Q_SELF
answer=My immediate team — we were in it together:A3_Q_TEAM
answer=A specific colleague who had it harder than me:A3_Q_WIDE
answer=The people our work ultimately serves:A3_Q_WIDE

nodes.text: When you were focused on your own challenge today — did anyone else's situation cross your mind?
options: Not really — I was heads-down
Briefly, but I had to focus on my own stuff
Yes — I noticed a colleague was struggling too
Yes — I thought about the people who'd be affected
answer=Not really — I was heads-down:A3_R_SELF_STRONG
answer=Briefly, but I had to focus on my own stuff:A3_R_SELF_MILD
answer=Yes — I noticed a colleague was struggling too:A3_R_TEAM
answer=Yes — I thought about the people who'd be affected:A3_R_WIDE

nodes.text: You thought of your team today. Did that awareness change anything about how you worked?
options: Yes — I checked in or offered help
Yes — I made a decision differently because of it
Not really — it was awareness, not action
I noticed it but was too stretched to act on it
answer=Yes — I checked in or offered help:A3_R_TEAM
answer=Yes — I made a decision differently because of it:A3_R_TEAM
answer=Not really — it was awareness, not action:A3_R_SELF_MILD
answer=I noticed it but was too stretched to act on it:A3_R_SELF_MILD

nodes.text: You were thinking about someone else or a broader purpose today. What did that feel like?
options: Grounding — it gave today more meaning
Heavy — their situation added to my stress
Motivating — it made me work harder
Fleeting — I noticed and then moved on
answer=Grounding — it gave today more meaning:A3_R_WIDE
answer=Motivating — it made me work harder:A3_R_WIDE
answer=Heavy — their situation added to my stress:A3_R_TEAM
answer=Fleeting — I noticed and then moved on:A3_R_TEAM

nodes.text: One last question about today: when you imagine someone who directly benefited from your work — who is that person?
options: Me — my growth, my goals
My manager or team lead
A colleague or peer
A customer, user, or someone downstream
answer=Me — my growth, my goals:SUMMARY_SELF
answer=My manager or team lead:SUMMARY_TEAM
answer=A colleague or peer:SUMMARY_TEAM
answer=A customer, user, or someone downstream:SUMMARY_WIDE

nodes.text: You were deep in your own work today — fully absorbed. That kind of focus has value. The question to hold lightly: who was depending on what you were building?

You noticed others but stayed focused on your own lane. There's wisdom in that — you can't pour from an empty cup. Just worth checking occasionally whether the cup is actually empty or just feels that way.
Your view today reached beyond just yourself — to your team, to a colleague, to something shared. That awareness is the beginning of something. Most meaningful work happens in that 'us' zone.

You were thinking about people outside your immediate circle today — a customer, an end user, someone who'd be affected. That kind of orientation doesn't just make work more meaningful. It makes it better work.

That's today. You showed up, you reflected, and you'll show up again tomorrow.

See you then.
